unit.comment: /* * Distributed under the Boost Software License, Version 1.0. (See accompanying * file LICENSE_1_0.txt or copy at http://www.boost.org/LICENSE_1_0.txt) */

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "INFO and WARN do not abort tests", "[messages][.]" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "#1455 - INFO and WARN can start with a linebreak", "[messages][.]" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "INFO gets logged on failure, even if captured before successful assertions", "[failing][messages][.]" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "Standard output from all sections is reported", "[messages][.]" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "The NO_FAIL macro reports a failure but does not fail the test", "[messages]" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "print unscoped info if passing unscoped info is printed", "[unscoped][info]" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "not prints unscoped info from previous failures", "[failing][.][unscoped][info]" )

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ( "prints unscoped info only for the first assertion", "[failing][.][unscoped][info]" )

unit.comment: // All the comma operators are side-effect free

diff:insert.comment: // Clang and gcc have different names for this warning, and clang also
// warns about an unused value. This warning must be disabled for C++20.

macro.name: TEST_CASE
macro.argument_list: ("CAPTURE can deal with complex expressions involving commas", "[messages][capture]")
